q: A capacitor is an electrical device that stores electrical energy in the form of an ___.
The capacitor was originally known as a ___.
A capacitor consists of two conductors separated by a ___ distance.
An ___ is inserted between its conductors.
Film capacitors are the ones that use plastic film as the ___ medium.
Ceramic capacitors are mainly used for high stability performance with low ___.
Electrolytic capacitors are the ones that use the ___ layer as the dielectric material.
There are mainly two types of electrolytic capacitors; ___ and aluminum.
The failure of electrolytic capacitors can be hazardous, resulting in an explosion or ___.
Variable capacitors mostly use ___ as the dielectric medium.
Variable capacitors are mostly used in AC circuits especially for high frequency, high power and low ___.
A supercapacitor (SC), also called an ___, is a high-capacity capacitor.
Film capacitors and ceramic capacitors are ___ capacitors which means that they do not have polarities.
Capacitors are used for blocking ___ current after getting fully charged.
Capacitors are also used to tune the frequency of ___ receivers.
Capacitors can also be used in a time-dependent circuit (___ circuit).
When a capacitor is connected to a battery the two conductors of the capacitor have charges of equal magnitude and ___ signs.
The magnitude of the charge on each conductor, $Q$, is called the ___ of the capacitor.
The potential difference between two conductors of the capacitor, $V$, is called the ___ of the capacitor.
The quantity of charge $Q$ on the capacitor is ___ proportional to the potential difference $V$ between the conductors.
The capacitance of a capacitor is defined as the ___ of the charge to the potential difference of the capacitor.
In the SI system, the unit of capacitance is ___.
If the potential difference of the capacitor is $1\text{ V}$ when it is given a charge $1\text{ C}$, its capacitance is ___.
The unit farad is named in honor of Michael ___.
In practice, typical devices have capacitances ranging from microfarad ($\mu\text{F}$) to ___.
When the charge on the capacitor is increased, the potential difference also increases ___.
Although the capacitance of a capacitor does not depend on $Q$ and $V$, it depends on the size and the ___ of the capacitor.
The amount of charge given to a conductor to change its potential by one unit is called the ___ of the conductor.
A ___ capacitor is the simplest capacitor.
Charging a capacitor means connecting the capacitor to a ___.
The surface charge density on each plate is $\sigma$ = ___.
If the plates are very close together, the electric field between the plates is ___.
Because the electric field between the plates is uniform, the magnitude of the potential difference $V$ between the plates equals ___.
For a given insulating material the capacitance of a parallel-plate capacitor is directly proportional to the ___ of its plates.
For a given insulating material the capacitance of a parallel-plate capacitor is inversely proportional to the ___ of the plates.
The permittivity $\epsilon$ of an insulating material is related to that of a vacuum $\epsilon_0$ as ___.
Here $\kappa$ is the dielectric constant or the ___ of the insulating material.
For a vacuum $\kappa = 1$ and for other insulating materials ___.
Thus, the dielectric constant of an insulating material is the ratio of its ___ to that of a vacuum.
If a parallel-plate capacitor has an insulating material between its plates, according to Eq. (9.6), its capacitance $C$ is ___.
If there is a vacuum between its plates, its capacitance $C_0$ is ___.
Therefore, the dielectric constant can be defined as the ratio of two ___ as follows.
When a charged capacitor is disconnected from a battery and the air medium between its plates is replaced by an insulating material, the ___ is found to decrease.
Since the charge $Q$ of the capacitor does not change, it indicates that the capacitance of the capacitor ___.
According to Eq. (9.9), dielectric constant can be expressed as the ratio of capacitances as well as the ratio of ___.
Insulating materials have values of dielectric constant $\kappa$ greater than ___.
A dielectric provides the following advantages: increase in maximum ___ voltage.
A dielectric provides the following advantages: possible mechanical support between the plates, which allows the plates to be close together without ___ contact.
A capacitor is similar to a rechargeable ___.
They are available in nearly any value and working voltages up to $1500\text{ V}$. They range from $10\%$ to ___ in tolerance.
In ceramic capacitors, one can develop both high capacitance and low capacitance by altering the ___ of the ceramic disc.
Electrolytic capacitors are the ones that use the oxide layer as the dielectric material. It has a wide ___ capacity.
Variable capacitors mostly use air as the dielectric medium. The capacitance can be ___ adjusted.
Supercapacitors are used in applications requiring many rapid charge/discharge cycles, rather than long-term compact energy storage in automobiles, buses, trains, cranes and ___.
Capacitors are used; as filters in power supplies and energy-storing devices in electronic ___ units.
If one conductor has a charge $+Q$ the other has a charge ___.
This relationship can be written as $Q = CV$ , where $C$ is called ___ of the capacitor.
The unit farad is a very ___ unit of capacitance.
Like a capacitor, if a conductor is given some charge its ___ will also change.
Suppose that an insulating material of permittivity $\epsilon$ is placed between the ___.
A ___ increases the maximum operating voltage of a capacitor even though the physical size doesn't change.
The dielectric may be able to withstand a larger ___ field between the plates than air.
The capacitance of a parallel-plate capacitor containing a dielectric is calculated using the formula $C = ___$.
When the distance between the two parallel plates of a capacitor is ___, its capacitance changes by 50 percent.
When an ___ material is inserted between the conductors of a capacitor, its capacitance increases.
A capacitor stores electrical energy in the form of an ___ field.
When the capacitor is charged, the charging process can be viewed as the apparent transfer of ___ between two conductors.
The charge has been transferred from a conductor at ___ potential to a conductor at higher potential.
___ has been done for such a transfer of charge from lower to higher potential.
The magnitude of the charge on the two conductors increases ___ during the charging process.
The energy stored by the capacitor can be analyzed from the ___ graph.
The work done in charging the capacitor is given by the shaded area under the ___.
The work done in charging the capacitor is equal to the area of the shaded ___ under the curve.
Before a capacitor is charged, the potential difference between two conductors is ___.
If the average potential difference before and after charging is $\bar{V}$, then $\bar{V} = ___$.
The work done $W$ for transferring charge of $Q$ between the two conductors is given by $W = ___$.
Since $C = Q/V$, the energy $W$ of the capacitor can be expressed as $W = ___$.
The third alternative expression for the energy $W$ of a capacitor is $W = ___$.
Placing a resistor in the charging circuit ___ down the charging process.
The greater the values of resistance and capacitance, the ___ it takes to charge the capacitor.
The voltage rises with time during the charging process according to the equation $V_c = ___$.
In the charging equation, $V_0$ is the voltage provided by the ___.
The product of the resistance $R$ and the capacitance $C$ is called the ___ constant of a capacitor.
The time constant $\tau$ is measured in ___.
After being charged, the capacitor can then be discharged through the ___ resistor $R_L$.
The voltage of the capacitor falls off with time according to the equation $V_c = ___$.
Several capacitors can be connected together to be used in a variety of ___.
There are two types of combination namely; (i) parallel and (ii) ___.
In a parallel combination, all the capacitors have the same ___ difference.
In a parallel combination, all the capacitors carry ___ amounts of charges.
If $Q$ is the total charge on the three parallel capacitors, then $Q = Q_1 + Q_2 + ___$.
A single capacitor that replaces a combination and maintains the same total charge and potential difference is called an ___ capacitor.
The equivalent capacitance of the capacitors connected in parallel is the ___ of the capacitances of the individual capacitors.
The equivalent capacitance $C$ of three capacitors in parallel is given by $C = ___$.
The equivalent capacitance $C_p$ of the parallel combination of $n$ identical capacitors, each of capacitance $C$, is $C_p = ___$.
Since the capacitors are connected in series each capacitor has the same ___.
Capacitors connected in series have different potential ___.
Suppose that $V$ is the total potential difference of the series capacitor combination, then $V = V_1 + V_2 + ___$.
When the capacitors are connected in series the ___ of the equivalent capacitance is the sum of the reciprocals of their individual capacitances.
The expression for the reciprocal of the equivalent capacitance of three series capacitors is $\frac{1}{C} = ___$.
The equivalent capacitance $C_s$ of the series combination of $n$ identical capacitors, each of capacitance $C$, is $C_s = ___$.
In which connection of the capacitors has each capacitor the same charge? ___
In which connection of the capacitors is the potential difference of each capacitor the same? ___
A ___ capacitor is the simplest capacitor.
A parallel-plate capacitor consists of two parallel metal plates separated by air or other ___ material.
The ratio of the capacitance of a capacitor with an insulating material to its capacitance with a vacuum is called the ___ constant.
If one conductor of a capacitor has a charge $+Q$ the other has a charge ___.
The charge of the capacitor is the ___ of the charge on each conductor.
The potential difference of the capacitor is the potential difference ___ two conductors of the capacitor.
Electric field intensity $E$ between the parallel plates is calculated by the formula $E = ___$.
The value $3.19 \times 10^{-6}\text{ C}$ can also be written as $3.19 \ ___$.
The constant symbol $\varepsilon_0$ stands for the ___ of free space.
The symbol $\kappa$ represents the ___ constant.
The abbreviation $\mu\text{F}$ stands for ___.
